title: Tesla’s Problem Is Opposite of Big Tech: Not Enough AI Spending
excerpt: After hearing Tesla Inc.’s seemingly countless promises about artificial intelligence, autonomous driving and robotics, Wall Street wants the company to start putting its money where its mouth is.
content_html:
AI

July 21, 2026 at 10:32 AM UTC

Updated on

July 21, 2026 at 8:05 PM UTC

After hearing Tesla Inc.’s seemingly countless promises about artificial intelligence, autonomous driving and robotics, Wall Street wants the company to start putting its money where its mouth is.

Elon Musk’s electric-vehicle maker has spent just $2.5 billion of the $25 billion in 2026 capital expenditures it forecast in April. The slow pace raises questions about whether Tesla’s spending enough to deliver the progress that AI-hungry investors are eager to see.
content_text: AIA Tesla Optimus robot hands out candy in New York last year.Photographer: Michael Nagle/BloombergJuly 21, 2026 at 10:32 AM UTCUpdated on July 21, 2026 at 8:05 PM UTCAfter hearing Tesla Inc.’s seemingly countless promises about artificial intelligence, autonomous driving and robotics, Wall Street wants the company to start putting its money where its mouth is.Elon Musk’s electric-vehicle maker has spent just $2.5 billion of the $25 billion in 2026 capital expenditures it forecast in April. The slow pace raises questions about whether Tesla’s spending enough to deliver the progress that AI-hungry investors are eager to see.

世界标准时间 2026 年 7 月 21 日上午 10:32

更新于 UTC 时间 2026 年 7 月 21 日晚上 8:05

在听到特斯拉公司关于人工智能、自动驾驶和机器人技术的看似无数的承诺后，华尔街希望该公司开始把钱说到做到。

埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的电动汽车制造商在 4 月份预测 2026 年资本支出为 250 亿美元，但仅支出了 25 亿美元。 缓慢的步伐引发了人们的疑问：特斯拉的支出是否足以实现渴望人工智能的投资者渴望看到的进展。
